tools.description: Inspect CodeStory readiness for the requested repository when diagnostics are needed.

inputSchema.description: Read readiness for one explicit repository.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: status

outputSchema.description: Compact capability state. Read codestory://status only when full diagnostics are needed.

next_action.description: Direct next action for the caller.

project.description: Requested repository root.

tools.description: Answer broad structural questions with repository evidence, sufficiency, truncation, and follow-up commands before source snippets. CodeStory prepares managed retrieval automatically.

inputSchema.description: Build a broad task packet with budget and sufficiency metadata.

extra_probes.description: Legacy string probes normalized through the same typed runtime resolver.

include_evidence.description: Include citation edge ids and score details.

latency_budget_ms.description: Optional retrieval latency budget in milliseconds.

probes.description: Optional tagged exact-path, symbol-id, file-symbol, free-query, or generation-bound continuation probes.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: packet

outputSchema.description: CodeStory broad task packet DTO with graph/sidecar evidence, budget truncation, unsafe-to-claim gaps, and follow-up commands.

budget.description: Budget limits, usage, and truncation metadata.

sufficiency.description: Covered claims, gaps, and follow-up contract.

tools.description: Discover candidate symbols and retrieval hits; for broad structural questions call packet before snippet/source reads. CodeStory prepares managed retrieval automatically.

inputSchema.description: Search indexed symbols and repo text.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: search

outputSchema.description: CodeStory discovery results DTO. Treat broad structural questions as packet-first; search rows select candidates for proof-bearing graph/source follow-up.

eligible_for_sufficiency.description: Whether this hit may satisfy answer-sufficiency requirements.

evidence_producer.description: Collector or retrieval producer that emitted the evidence.

evidence_tier.description: Evidence provenance tier. structural_text is collector-backed source-range evidence, not parser-backed graph coverage.

links.description: Bounded continuation resource links for this hit.

probe.description: Optional generation-bound continuation probe for packet reuse.

match_quality.description: How exactly the hit matched the query: exact, normalized_exact, prefix, fuzzy, semantic_suggestion, or repo_text.

resolution_status.description: Resolution state. source_range_only has a source span but no typed graph resolution.

resolvable.description: Whether the hit can be used as a symbol target.

query_assessment.description: Exactness, weak-hit, repo-text diagnostic, and next-action assessment.

repo_text_enabled.description: Whether repo text search was enabled.

repo_text_stats.description: Repo text scan cap, byte, and truncation telemetry.

tools.description: Return a compact repository map for orientation before packet/search; equivalent to codestory://grounding. The first call may refresh the local map and begin managed retrieval preparation.

inputSchema.description: Return the same compact repository orientation as codestory://grounding.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: ground

outputSchema.description: CodeStory grounding snapshot DTO for compact repository orientation.

tools.description: List indexed files and coverage from a locally fresh index; refreshes the repository map before dispatch and does not wait for broad search.

inputSchema.description: List indexed files from the existing local index.

language.description: Only include files for this language.

path.description: Only include files whose path contains this text.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

role.description: Only include files with this inferred role.

tools.name: files

outputSchema.description: Indexed file inventory and coverage summary.

complete.description: Whether indexing completed for this file.

usable.description: Whether the index has usable files.

tools.description: Analyze one explicit path source against the last complete local index while preserving bounded stale and error evidence. Cold or partial state may trigger managed indexing before dispatch. Prefer paths, use changed_paths for compatibility or change_records for status-rich input. Never discovers git changes and does not wait for broad search.

inputSchema.description: Analyze exactly one explicit path source against the last complete local index.

change_records.description: Changed file records with path, kind, optional status, and optional previous_path.

previous_path.description: Optional previous path accepted only for renamed or copied records; it can seed bounded proxy graph evidence when the current path is not indexed.

status.description: Optional raw git-style status such as M, A, D, R100, C100, or ??.

changed_paths.description: Compatibility alias for project-relative paths to analyze.

filter.description: Optional impacted-symbol filter by path or display-name substring.

paths.description: Preferred simple input: project-relative paths to analyze.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: affected

outputSchema.description: Changed-file impact analysis DTO from the last complete local index.

previous_path.description: Optional previous path accepted only for renamed or copied records; it can seed bounded proxy graph evidence when the current path is not indexed.

status.description: Optional raw git-style status such as M, A, D, R100, C100, or ??.

completeness.description: Completeness, direct/propagated counts, confidence, and truncation evidence.

complete.description: Whether a complete impact claim is supported.

truncated.description: Whether runtime or transport bounds capped evidence.

truncation_reasons.description: Field-specific runtime and transport truncation reasons.

unavailable_evidence_count.description: Inputs whose absence could not be classified more strongly.

counts.description: Original result counts before response caps.

follow_ups.description: Evidence-derived follow-up actions with optional structured invocations.

matched_files.description: Changed paths matched to indexed files.

complete.description: Whether indexing completed for this file.

truncated.description: Whether any result collection was capped.

uncovered_inputs.description: All inputs without complete graph evidence, including malformed indexed files.

unmatched_paths.description: Changed paths that did not match indexed file identity.

items.description: Input path that did not match indexed file identity.

tools.description: Return a focused source snippet after packet, search, or graph evidence selects a concrete target.

inputSchema.description: Resolve a symbol and return bounded line or function-body source context.

choose.description: Resolve by the 1-based alternative number from an ambiguity error.

context.description: Surrounding context lines above and below the selected source range.

function_body.description: CLI-compatible scope selector; true requests `function_body`, false requests `line_context`.

project.description: Absolute repository root for this request. The MCP server is multi-project and does not retain a global workspace binding.

tools.name: snippet

fallback_reason.description: Reason function-body selection fell back to line context, when applicable.

range_source.description: Source of the selected function-body range, when available.

snippet_truncated.description: Whether the snippet hit a byte cap.

truncation_guidance.description: Follow-up guidance when the snippet hit its byte cap.

bookmark.description: Saved bookmark id to build context around.

id.description: Stable node id to build context around.

include_evidence.description: Include citation edge ids and score details.

evidence_tier.description: Evidence provenance tier. structural_text is collector-backed source-range evidence, not parser-backed graph coverage.

origin.description: Citation origin, such as indexed_symbol or text_match.

resolution_status.description: Resolution state. source_range_only has a source span but no typed graph resolution.

resolvable.description: Whether the citation can be resolved as a symbol.
